reviews.review_msg: Easy class if u read the textbook but not easy to get high mark. Class is not very interesting but there is a attendance mark so should go to class.

I LOVE YOU MR SOSA !!!!! His free-style teaching method really entertained the whole class. His tests were extremely easy if you followed the lecture. Take home final was a little brutal but it worth the effort at the end! GREAT PROF EVER!!!

He's wonderful.  I've never had another prof who is as engaged and engaging as Prof Sosa.  His class made me want to do more phonetics, but I've found, in upper level courses, that nothing and no one else can equal his interest and enthusiasm.
If you're a good writer, 309 is an incredibly easy class and he's easy to please.

Professor Sosa is one interesting man, he goes out of his way to encourage class discussion in a class without tutorials. But his material comes directly from the textbook and the take home midterms are EXTREMELY long (10-11 essay questions each) so if you take his class make sure you have plenty of time to write them the week before. Great class:)

In my four years at SFU, this is the only class that I've attended every single lecture.   (Not even swine flu kept me away!) I love Dr. Sosa. He's extremely knowledgeable, funny, engaging, and has a very pleasant speaking voice(Venezuelan, educated in Britain).  His class changed the way I look at spoken language. I would recommend him forever. <3

I really don't want to seem biased because he gave me a bad mark but I genuinely think he is a terrible professor. He's very unpredictable as you don't know what to expect on exams...at all. His marking seems unfair and my final mark just didn't seem to add up. I just don't think he is professional at what he does.

he seems to be very interesting and entertaining during class but his midterms are hardcore. also, the way he grades and unfair and he doesn't give you a chance to rechange your marks because he doesn't believe in the textbook answers.

The course is easy to understand.  Midterms are fair because they are take home exam.  Boring class.  He likes picking on favours and only knows their names.  Don't really like him but it's a mark booster course
This course, you should def take!! He's very entertaining and everything learnt from this class is/will be useful at least once in your life time haha. I think he's a genius. lol

Very entertaining teaching style, e.g. uses accents to illustrate phonetic concepts. Encourages engagement in phonetic practice, especially from those with non-English backgrounds (I wish they weren't always so shy!) Generous marker, easy assignments, fair exams, and a great guy to chat with. I highly recommend him if yr into phonetics/phonology.
His condescending attitude toward non native born speakers is so obvious which makes me feel I am not part of the class.
he's awesome! he lets you stalk him at home hahaha.ps he gave me his number ;)

Experienced phonetician,BUT certainly NOT a good teacher.Sytle:funny but pointless,unhelpful.Lecture:Poorly organized and mostly not correlated with the exams. Personality: Always pick favorites(too obvious)He is the worst ling prof because he scaled students according to his own impression.He doesn't "teach", you have to find the answers yourself!

He is one of the best professors in teaching phonology, he is so interested in this particular area. Exams were not hard, he really gives marks, always go to his office and ask questions, he is so helpful. He is a GREAT person.

Entertaining and interesting lectures, but if you want to do well in this class you've got to be highly self-motivated, cause it's -tough-.  There's a lot of stuff on exams that he only mentioned in passing, so you've got to be paying very close attention.

I thought he was an easy marker. He gives part marks for answers that are somewhat related to the correct answer. The midterms were all straight from his lectures.  If you attend all his lectures, you'll be fine. He does pick favorites, but it's not too bad. He won't take marks off from those who isn't one of his favorites.
He is a retard!!!!! The mid-terms are ridiculously unrelated to the lectures!!! You get good grades depends if he likes you or not...seriously. He is a disgrace to the commuity of teaching.
No Comments

omg, seriously avoid him at all cost. His markings are extremely hard...he put questions on the quizzes and exams that are not even related to what we're suppose to study or learn.  Not to mention, whenever you try to ask him a question during lecture...most of the time you'll end up with the answer "Huh, wtf did he just said?"
No Comments
The worst prof.in my whole life.. just avoid his class is the smartest way..
Emotional scarring will fade away, but the big Fat F on your transcript won't.
In all my years at SFU, I have never disliked any professor other than him.  Bad teaching. Bad organization.  Bad attitude towards students.. Avoid at all cost.

Redundant (3 hours to say what a better prof could more clearly articulate in 1); notes: paragraphs, often from the text; open-book midterms: good if don't mind paying $500 to reword the text; final exam: too open-ended, but gave extra time; helpful in office hrs. Overall, good for GPA, but complicates and makes tedious an otherwise simple course.

He is one of the best prof, he is really interested in languages and the different sounds that people can produce,  I really loved his class. the exams are not easy, u have to understand his lecture, not to memorize everything in the book.
No Comments
No Comments
No Comments
...he's freaken unorganized...says he'll return out quiz's b4 the MT...never returned them...he doesn't know and or remember what he says or teaches during lec...

he's simply not good in teaching.he surely discourages people.of course, he doesn't know the material, therefore he can't explain it well.his questions from exams are awfully hard and confusing.he ONLY likes those students that he favors.worst and very first prof that i have taken class with.
No Comments

Too bad he is already a professor at SFU. So no matter how bad his course teaching evaluation is he will still be at SFU.. But I sincerely hope to him get fired from SFU as soon as his 10 years contract expire. He is totally useless at teaching... time to retire and stop torturing students.
Just read the comments below.

Don't take a class from him if u don't need to. As many had said, it was a torture. He had strong prejudice and would make fun of u if u had low marks in tests. A lot of little quizzes and they are very hard. Very difficult to get a good grade if he doesn't like you. Poor teaching quality too.

I seriously hope the faculty of Lingustic department would consider fire some of the useless profssors at SFU.  Just because they have been teaching at SFU for a long time does not mean they can teach at all.. This is the worst course taken at SFU ever.

I warn u guys!! Never ever take any course with this guy.He is awfully disorganized.He only makes fun of u and prejudges u.Never ask any question cuz it's useless!You'll never get to an answer simply because he doesn't even know it himself.The most boring class I've ever had.
He gives very discouraging comments, and he expects a lot from exams.

Sosa is a very amusing prof, in the way that he can always bring a smile to your face. However you are never given enough time for the exams & they always have irrelevent ?'s on them. If you go to his office hrs he will go out of his way to help you.
The worst prof. in the whole ling. department.  Not helpful AT ALL!!!!  Play favorites...... don't know how to teach.
The worst teacher I have ever had. He simply can not teach. He should not be teaching. He discouraged me to further take any ling. classes.
He IS the worst prof I've ever had!! No more to say.. Just don't take this course with this guy!! Seriously this course was a nightmare cuz of him~!!

Horrible horrible teacher. Can't teach, not engaging, poor organization, etc. On top of that he is unfriendly, intolerant, and appears prejudice.  Picked poor text for the course which he admitted to be dense and dull. Do not take this course w/ him.
exam is so hard

This professor is the worst professor that I have ever met!!  He is not helpful, he play favourites, and when you do bad on an exam he makes a face AND THEN hand you the exam!  He made me lose my interest in Ling. and give up on getting my TESL cert.
I feel sorry for the guy but he just can't teach.
He is the worst prof I had at SFU. Can't believe he is still teaching!!!

He's profession in linguistic, but he's not a good professor. He's lazy. He announced to give us back the assignments since the beginning of class, but he'd never do it. He didn't give us hints about what'd the exams be. He confused me and my friends.

socioling, an interesting course listening to his experience in doing research. mt is open bk and actually his 321 mt n final are open bk as well. so dont worry abut memorization. u just need to go over before exam, so that look them up fast on the exam.

Sosa makes a simple class exceedingly unclear, then expects you to write the textbook for your exams. Exams were based more on memorization of his unclear theories than on problem solving. Worst linguistics prof at SFU. Sorry Sosa.
I personally hate phonetics, but he made it fun and INTERESTING damn it.  He actually had intriguing lectures and didn't just regurgitate the text.  If you want what the text says, just READ IT.
No Comments
You have to figure things out yourself, don't expect him to teach !!
No Comments
useless and can't learn anything from his lecture
boring lectures..  but you'll learn a bit..
No Comments
excellent liguist, but poor prof
No Comments
Great instructor!
No Comments
Better Left Unsaid
it's an interesting course but the take home exams are not easy
Great prof. Hard course, but he teaches it very well and he makes it fun.
this is a core course, but don' t take with this professor!!!!
very knowledgeable but doesn't prepare too much
Good prof, fun lectures.
I suggest don't take his class unless it is necessary
